# Feature: proof-pytest

> Scope: scripts/proof/pytest_purlin.py

## What it does

Pytest plugin that collects `@pytest.mark.proof(feature, proof_id, rule_id)` markers during test execution and emits feature-scoped proof JSON files alongside the corresponding spec files.

## Rules

- RULE-1: Tests marked with `@pytest.mark.proof("feature", "PROOF-N", "RULE-N")` are collected during the `call` phase only
- RULE-2: Each proof entry records feature, id, rule, test_file (relative path), test_name, status (pass/fail), and tier
- RULE-3: Proof files are written to `specs/<feature_dir>/<feature>.proofs-<tier>.json`, where feature_dir is discovered by scanning `specs/**/*.md`
- RULE-4: When a feature has no matching spec file, proofs default to the `specs/` directory
- RULE-5: On session finish, old proof entries for the same feature are purged before writing new ones (ghost cleanup)

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): A passing test with `@pytest.mark.proof` produces a proof entry with status "pass"
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): The emitted proof JSON entry contains all required fields: feature, id, rule, test_file, test_name, status, tier
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Proof file is written next to the matching spec file in the correct directory
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): A proof for an unknown feature writes to `specs/<feature>.proofs-default.json`
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Running tests twice replaces old entries for the feature, not appending duplicates
